page: Amazon Web Services i2.8xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This configuration provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The server is equipped with 244.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.62 GB per core. For storage-intensive workloads, it includes 6400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the i2.8xlarge in the average performance tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The system achieved a multi-core Geekbench score of 5071.0 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 27789.21. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 1139.29 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 82.93 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with a Geekbench single-core score of 534.0 and a text processing score of 1084.0, both falling into the poor category. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register as weak.

Given its substantial 6400 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 244.0 GB of RAM, the i2.8xlarge is designed for storage-heavy and database workloads. Passmark database operations scored 12597.61, and Redis SET operations reached 11.15 million operations per second, confirming its suitability for transactional databases, NoSQL systems, and caching layers. While its older Intel Xeon processor limits its efficiency for modern single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM inference, its high local storage density provides qualitative cost efficiency for legacy data-serving applications that require high capacity without relying on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services i2.8xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server running on the Xen hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 244.0 GB of RAM and 6400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5071.0 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 27789.21. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 534.0. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-capacity local storage requirements, making it suitable for database operations, caching layers, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i2.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 244 GB RAM, and 6400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database workloads and high-capacity local data processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i2.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 244 GB RAM, and 6400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSDs and balanced memory for data-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2
6400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
244.0 GB of system memory with 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance suitable for database operations and Redis caching
Lower single-core performance limits efficiency for single-threaded tasks
